summary: Addresses all 3 sourcery-ai[bot] review #1 feedback items: (1) REPO_ROOT computation + cd in ci-deploy-operator.sh and ci-run-tempest.sh for arbitrary working directory support, (2) explicit two-stage admin password extraction guard with ::error:: messages in ci-run-tempest.sh, (3) command -v flux guard in ci-dump-diagnostics.sh to suppress noisy 'command not found' output. All scripts pass shellcheck --severity=warning. Changes are minimal and correctly scoped.

tests_checklist.item: shellcheck --severity=warning passes on all 3 modified hack/ scripts
No functional change to CI behavior — scripts produce identical results

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing code patterns (SPDX header, set -euo pipefail, CC-0050 reference)
REPO_ROOT computation matches sibling script pattern (ci-build-service-image.sh)
Admin password extraction uses two-stage validation (secret existence + key presence)
flux guard uses idiomatic command -v pattern

security_checklist.item: No secrets exposed in logs or error messages
Admin password extraction failure messages do not leak secret values
No new shell injection vectors introduced

architecture_checklist.item: Changes are minimal and address only the requested feedback
No scope creep beyond the 3 feedback items

fail_fast_checklist.item: ci-run-tempest.sh fails fast with ::error:: on missing secret
ci-run-tempest.sh fails fast with ::error:: on missing password key
ci-deploy-operator.sh validates OPERATOR format before use

defensive_checklist.item: kubectl failure captured and reported with actionable error message
Empty base64 value detected before base64 -d
flux CLI checked for existence before invocation

suggested_improvements: guard-shell-commands-against-empty-variable-expansion.md: The CC-0050 example (lines 23-26) is about REPO_ROOT/working directory, which is a different concern than the pattern's stated purpose (empty variable expansion in Dockerfile ARG/ENV passed to package managers). Consider moving this example to a separate pattern or removing it to avoid confusing future reviewers.

add-explicit-checks-before-using-extracted-values-under-set-e.md: The 'What to check' section references 'KEYSTONE_ENDPOINT at line 14-17' which does not exist in ci-run-tempest.sh (the script has CATALOG_SVC and SERVICE_K8S_NAME). Update the pattern to reference the correct variable names.

ci-build-service-image.sh uses ${REPO_ROOT}/path prefix style (no cd) while ci-deploy-operator.sh and ci-run-tempest.sh use cd + relative paths. Both work, but standardizing on one approach would improve consistency. Not flagged as an issue since ci-build-service-image.sh is not in the diff scope.
